description: Preservation order that suspends automated deletion of specific communications, files, and user data pending litigation, regulatory investigation, or legal request, overriding any data retention...

actors.name: Legal Administrator
actors.description: Creates and manages legal holds; defines custodians and date ranges

actors.name: System Administrator
actors.description: Grants legal admin access; configures hold storage destinations

actors.name: Retention System
actors.description: Checks hold status before deleting any content

fields.name: hold_id
fields.label: Unique identifier for this legal hold

fields.name: name
fields.label: Human-readable label describing the legal matter

fields.name: custodian_user_ids
fields.label: Array of user IDs whose content is subject to preservation

fields.name: channel_ids
fields.label: Optional array of channel IDs to scope the hold; empty means all channels for th

fields.name: start_at
fields.label: Earliest message timestamp covered by this hold (inclusive)

fields.name: end_at
fields.label: Latest message timestamp covered by this hold (inclusive); null means ongoing

fields.name: include_files
fields.label: Whether file attachments sent by custodians are also preserved

fields.name: export_path
fields.label: Storage destination where preserved content is archived for legal review

values.name: active
values.description: Hold is in force; retention deletion is blocked for covered content

values.name: released
values.description: Hold lifted; content is again subject to normal retention policies

transitions.description: Legal matter resolved; legal admin releases the hold

rules.rule_01: An active legal hold overrides any data retention policy; covered content must not be deleted regardless of its age.
rules.rule_02: Content covered by a hold is determined by the intersection of custodian identities, channel scope, and date range.
rules.rule_03: When the hold is released, covered content immediately becomes subject to the applicable retention policy; content already past the retention window must be evaluated for immediate deletion.
rules.rule_04: A hold does not affect the users' ability to continue messaging; they are not notified that a hold exists unless required by law.
rules.rule_05: Holds are logged in the audit trail with the identity of the legal administrator who created or released them.
rules.rule_06: Multiple holds may overlap; content is preserved as long as at least one active hold covers it.
rules.rule_07: File attachments are included only when include_files is true; the hold is independently configurable for messages and files.
rules.rule_08: The hold preservation archive is stored in a separate, access-controlled location independent of the primary message store.
rules.rule_09: Releasing a hold should trigger a review to determine whether retained content must be deleted to comply with data retention obligations.

then.description: Hold record created; retention system begins excluding covered content from deletion

hold_created.result: All covered content is immediately protected from any automated or manual deletion

given: retention job or administrator attempts to delete content
content is within the date range and custodian scope of at least one active hold
deletion_blocked_by_hold.result: Deletion skipped for held content; other non-held content proceeds normally

given: legal administrator requests export of held content

then.description: Preserved content packaged into archive at export_path

hold_content_exported.result: Archive of held content available for legal review

hold_released.result: Content previously held is now subject to normal retention policies

given: one hold covering content is released
at least one other active hold still covers the same content
overlapping_hold_maintained.result: Content remains preserved because the remaining hold is still active

errors.message: One or more specified users do not exist.
This hold has already been released.
Legal hold requires an enterprise compliance license.

events.name: legal_hold.created
events.description: A new legal hold was placed

events.name: legal_hold.deletion_blocked
events.description: Retention deletion was skipped because content is under a legal hold

events.name: legal_hold.exported
events.description: Held content packaged into an export archive for legal review

related.reason: Legal holds override retention deletion; the two systems must coordinate
Held content can be exported in compliance formats for eDiscovery
All hold creation, release, and export operations are recorded in the audit log

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state
